title: Donnellsmithia biennis

The classification of Donnellsmithia biennis places it in the kingdom Plantae, further categorized into the phylum Magnoliophyta, and the class Magnoliopsida. Within the class Magnoliopsida, the order Apiales and family Apiaceae are its respective order and family classifications. Further classification includes its genus and species, making it Donnellsmithia biennis.

intro: Donnellsmithia biennis is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as a wild parsnip or poison parsnip. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing pain, fighting infections, and protecting against oxidative stress.
